e: Navštívit is a Czech verb meaning 'to visit'. Example: 'Minulý rok jsme navštívili toto muzeum' (Last year we visited this museum).
Cestovat is a Czech verb meaning 'to travel'. Example: 'Ona cestovala do Francie minulý týden' (She traveled to France last week).
Sama is a Czech adjective meaning 'alone'. Example: 'Minulý rok cestoval sám v USA' (Last year he traveled alone in the USA).
Včera is a Czech adverb meaning 'yesterday'. Example: 'On navštívil včera dvě muzea' (He visited two museums yesterday).
Do zahraničí is a Czech phrase meaning 'abroad' or 'to a foreign country'. Example: 'Já jsem nikdy nebyl v zahraničí' (I have never been abroad).
Plánovat is a Czech verb meaning 'to plan'. Example: 'Já budu plánovat mou dovolenou zítra' (I will plan my vacation tomorrow).
Rozhodnout is a Czech verb meaning 'to decide'. Example: 'My jsme se ještě nerozhodli kam jít' (We haven't decided yet where to go).
Dovolená is a Czech noun meaning 'vacation' or 'holiday'. Example: 'On si ještě neplánoval svou dovolenou' (He hasn't planned his vacation yet).
Výlet is a Czech noun meaning 'trip' or 'excursion', often a short recreational journey. Example: 'Ona si ještě neplánovala svůj výlet' (She hasn't planned her trip yet).
Pronajmout is a Czech verb meaning 'to rent'. Example: 'Už jsem plánoval pronajmout si auto' (I've already planned to rent a car).
Turista is a Czech noun meaning 'tourist'. Example: 'Já jsem turista' (I am a tourist).
Místo is a Czech noun meaning 'place' or 'location'. Example: 'Které místo bych měl navštívit?' (Which place should I visit?).

s: nechat si ujít means 'to miss' in Czech
e: Nechat si ujít is a Czech phrase meaning 'to miss out on' or 'not to miss'. Example: 'Neměl by sis nechat ujít toto místo' (You shouldn't miss this place).
Vstupné is a Czech noun meaning 'entrance fee' or 'admission charge'. Example: 'Kolik musím zaplatit za vstupné?' (How much must I pay for the entrance fee?).
Obrázek is a Czech noun meaning 'picture' or 'image'. Example: 'Tento obrázek je velmi krásný' (This picture is very beautiful).

s: pořídit fotku means 'to take photos' in Czech
e: Pořídit fotku is a Czech phrase meaning 'to take a photo' or 'to take pictures'. Example: 'Zde můžete pořídit fotky' (You can take photos here).
Povolit is a Czech verb meaning 'to allow' or 'to permit'. Example: 'Pořitování fotek zde není povoleno' (Taking photos is not allowed here).

s: dotýkat se means 'to touch' in Czech
e: Dotýkat se is a Czech verb meaning 'to touch'. Example: 'Nemáte povoleno dotýkat se obrázků' (You are not allowed to touch the pictures).
Průvodce is a Czech noun meaning 'guide' or 'tour guide'. Example: 'Průvodce povolil turistům pořizování fotek' (The guide allowed tourists to take photos).
Hotel is a Czech noun (loan word from English) meaning 'a hotel' or 'lodging establishment'. Example: 'My máme rádi tento hotel' (We like this hotel).
Zůstat is a Czech verb meaning 'to stay' or 'to remain'. Example: 'My zde chceme zůstat na dvě noci' (We want to stay here for two nights).

s: přihlášení na hotel means 'check-in' in Czech
e: Přihlášení na hotel is a Czech phrase meaning 'check-in' at a hotel. Example: 'Přihlášení na hotel je po 14 hod.' (Hotel check-in is after 2 PM).

s: odhlášení z hotelu means 'check-out' in Czech
e: Odhlášení z hotelu is a Czech phrase meaning 'check-out' from a hotel. Example: 'Odhlášení z hotelu je do 15:00' (Hotel check-out is by 3 PM).
Recepce is a Czech noun meaning 'reception' or 'reception desk'. Example: 'Recepce je otevřená 24 hodin denně' (Reception is open 24 hours a day).
Host is a Czech noun meaning 'guest' or 'visitor'. Example: 'Na recepci čeká pět hostů na přihlášení na hotel' (Five guests are waiting at reception for check-in).
Rezervovat is a Czech verb meaning 'to reserve' or 'to book'. Example: 'Já chci rezervovat dvě místnosti pro čtyři osoby' (I want to reserve two rooms for four people).

s: jednolůžkový pokoj means 'a single room' in Czech
e: Jednolůžkový pokoj is a Czech phrase meaning 'a single room' (with one bed). Example: 'Já chci rezervovat jednolůžkový pokoj na jednu noc' (I want to reserve a single room for one night).

s: pokoj pro dva means 'a double room' in Czech
e: Pokoj pro dva is a Czech phrase meaning 'a room for two' or 'a double room'. Example: 'Já chci rezervovat dvoulůžkový pokoj na jeden týden' (I want to reserve a double room for one week).
Přistýlka is a Czech noun meaning 'extra bed' or 'cot'. Example: 'Já chci přistýlku pro mou dceru' (I want an extra bed for my daughter).

s: společný pokoj means 'a shared room' in Czech
e: Společný pokoj is a Czech phrase meaning 'a shared room' or 'common room'. Example: 'Tento hotel nabízí společné pokoje' (This hotel offers shared rooms).
Zařízení is a Czech noun meaning 'facility' or 'equipment'. Example: 'V hotelu je mnoho zařízení' (The hotel has many facilities).

s: klíč od pokoje means 'a room key' in Czech
e: Klíč od pokoje is a Czech phrase meaning 'a room key' or 'hotel key'. Example: 'Mohu nechat klíč od pokoje na recepci?' (Can I leave the room key at reception?).
Zavazadlo is a Czech noun meaning 'luggage' or 'baggage'. Example: 'Mohu zde nechat své zavazadlo?' (Can I leave my luggage here?).

q: What Czech word refers to the worldwide computer network?
e: Internet is a Czech noun (loan word) meaning 'Internet'. Example: 'Poskytuje hotel internet?' (Does the hotel provide internet?).

s: nouzový východ means 'emergency exit' in Czech
e: Nouzový východ is a Czech phrase meaning 'emergency exit'. Example: 'Nouzový východ je zde' (The emergency exit is here).
Pokojská is a Czech noun meaning 'maid' or 'room attendant'. Example: 'Tato pokojská zde pracuje' (This maid works here).
Odpočívat is a Czech verb meaning 'to rest' or 'to relax'. Example: 'Chci odpočívat ve svém pokoji' (I want to rest in my room).
Uklízet is a Czech verb meaning 'to clean' or 'to tidy up'. Example: 'Pokojská uklízí pokoj' (The maid cleans the room).

s: vstupní hala means 'the lobby' in Czech
e: Vstupní hala is a Czech phrase meaning 'the lobby' or 'the entrance hall'. Example: 'Vaše zavazadlo je ve vstupní hale' (Your luggage is in the lobby).
Ukázat is a Czech verb meaning 'to show' or 'to display'. Example: 'Prosím, ukažte svůj cestovní pas' (Please show your passport).
Cestovní pas is a Czech phrase meaning 'passport'. Example: 'Prosím, ukažte svůj cestovní pas' (Please show your passport).
Vízum is a Czech noun meaning 'visa' or 'entry permit'. Example: 'Prosím, ukažte své vízum' (Please show your visa).

s: průkaz totožnosti means 'Personal ID card' in Czech
e: Průkaz totožnosti is a Czech phrase meaning 'personal ID card' or 'identity card'. Example: 'Prosím, ukažte svůj průkaz totožnosti' (Please show your ID card).
Položit is a Czech verb meaning 'to put' or 'to place'. Example: 'On položí tašku na stůl' (He will put the bag on the table).

s: pásový dopravník means 'conveyor belt' in Czech
e: Pásový dopravník is a Czech phrase meaning 'conveyor belt'. Example: 'On dal jeho tašku na pásový dopravník' (He put his bag on the conveyor belt).

s: vyzout means 'to take off' in Czech
e: Vyzout is a Czech verb meaning 'to take off' (particularly shoes). Example: 'Já jsem si vyzul své kožené boty' (I took off my leather shoes).
Košík is a Czech noun meaning 'basket' or 'small basket'. Example: 'Oni položili své hodinky do košíku' (They put their watches in the basket).

s: palubní vstupenka means 'boarding pass' in Czech
e: Palubní vstupenka is a Czech phrase meaning 'boarding pass' for airline travel. Example: 'Já jsem položil svůj palubní vstupenku do košíku' (I put my boarding pass in the basket).
Záměr is a Czech noun meaning 'purpose' or 'intention'. Example: 'On nemá záměr sem přijít' (He has no intention to come here).
Prohlížení památek is a Czech phrase meaning 'sightseeing' or 'visiting historic sites'. Example: 'Na prohlídku památek' (For a sightseeing tour).
